sections.paragraph:
ἀνόμημα, ατος, τό (s. ἀνομέω; Stoic. III 136; Diod S 1, 77, 2; 17, 5, 4; PGM 4, 3099; LXX; En 9:10; TestDan 1:9; TestGad 2:5 [v.l. ἀνομία]; Jos., Ant. 8, 251; CSchmidt and WSchubart, Altchr. Texte 1910, p. 111) lawless action, iniquity w. ἁμαρτία Hv 1, 3, 1; w. ἀνομία and ἁμαρτία 1 Cl 18:2f (Ps 50:3f).

Stoic Stoic , various dates—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

En En = I Enoch—List 2

TestDan TestDan = Testament of Dan, s. Test12Patr—List 2

TestGad TestGad = Testament of Gad, s. Test12Patr—List 2

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Hv Hv = Visions

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 85
